Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Comprehensive C++ Hashmap Benchmarks (2022) 25 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 69 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 337 upvotes, 61 comments
  4. 15 Years of Forking 232 upvotes, 43 comments
  5. 15 years, one server, 8GB RAM and 500k users – how Webminal refuses to die 189 upvotes, 37 comments
  6. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 41 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 257 upvotes, 60 comments
  8. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 928 upvotes, 275 comments
  9. Hardware Image Compression 45 upvotes, 8 comments
  10. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 83 upvotes, 21 comments
  11. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 598 upvotes, 225 comments
  12. How to Turn Anything into a Router 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 47 upvotes, 13 comments
  14. How A Spartan Revolutionized Baseball 18 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. VHDL's Crown Jewel 90 upvotes, 32 comments
  16. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 321 upvotes, 148 comments
  17. I use excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 145 upvotes, 68 comments
  18. Spring Boot Done Right: Lessons from a 400-Module Codebase 22 upvotes, 6 comments
  19. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 781 upvotes, 500 comments
  20. Show HN: Phantom – Open-source AI agent on its own VM that rewrites its config 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 123 upvotes, 109 comments
  23. Coding agents could make free software matter again 225 upvotes, 219 comments
  24. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 270 upvotes, 270 comments
  25. Ghostmoon.app – The Swiss Army Knife for your macOS menu bar 86 upvotes, 76 comments
  26. Parrots pack twice as many neurons as primate brains of the same mass 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  27. How the AI Bubble Bursts 188 upvotes, 207 comments
  28. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 278 upvotes, 321 comments
  29. Eclipse GlassFish: This Isn't Your Father's GlassFish 33 upvotes, 30 comments
  30. The First Video Game Was Just a Box in the Corner of a Bar 20 upvotes, 16 comments
